title: rule.schema.json v1.1.0 — authority + evidenceClass + instanceLocation
description: Extends rule.schema.json from v1.0.0 to v1.1.0 with three additive non-breaking changes: top-level authority block (required, matches deliverable.schema.json shape, seeds 5-tier auth model), evidenceClass on transitions (required where to is formalized or automated), and instanceLocation convention documented in schema description (rule instances live at schemas/_instances/rules/). First deliverable authored prospectively as a schema-bound instance from initiation.

outputs.description: schemas/rule.schema.json v1.0.0 → v1.1.0 — additive changes: top-level authority block (required), evidenceClass on transitions (required where to is formalized/automated), instanceLocation convention in schema description.
acceptanceCriteria: Validates as Draft 7 via ajv-cli@5 compile --strict=false
authority block present in properties with author, approver, revertAuthority all required string fields
authority listed in top-level required array
transitions items now define evidenceClass property
transitions items have conditional requirement that evidenceClass is required when to is formalized or automated (via if/then)
Top-level description field updated to document instanceLocation convention

outputs.description: schemas/_instances/deliverables/d-2026-06-01-rule-schema-v1.1-additive.json — prospective deliverable instance authored at S4-authored, transitions through S5-in-progress and S6-staged across the dispatch execution.
acceptanceCriteria: Instance file exists at canonical path
lifecycleState transitions reflect actual execution (S4 → S5 → S6 by PR open; S7 → S8 in post-merge amendment)

outputs.description: schema-refs.json regenerated — rule entry version 1.0.0 → 1.1.0; auto-changelog entry; no other changes.
acceptanceCriteria: meta.changelog has new entry with date and 'Updated: rule v1.0.0 -> v1.1.0' change
Zero changes to other registered entries

preMerge: refs rebuild --check exit 0 after rule schema version bump and refs regeneration

transitions.trigger: Dispatch authored after D-1 close; spec instance authored before any code change (prospective pattern)

notes: Third deliverable in the rule-pipeline foundation arc, first authored prospectively as a schema-bound instance (D-0 and D-1 were retro-backfilled into the deliverable schema). Additive-only changes ensure backward compatibility with rule.schema.json v1.0.0 — no existing field constraints tightened, no fields removed. Authority block shape matches deliverable.schema.json exactly so the 5-tier auth enum promotion (when authored) updates both schemas in one PR. evidenceClass conditional requirement uses JSON Schema if/then to enforce only on promotion-direction transitions; downgrade transitions (formalized → working-model, formalized → reverted) do not require it. Dispatch named the deliverable id 'd-2026-06-01-rule-schema-v1.1-additive' and branch 'feat/rule-schema-v1.1-additive-2026-06-01'; both normalized to remove dots (v1.1 → v11) because deliverable.schema.json id and scope.branch patterns require [a-z0-9-]+ (same issue as followup-register instance in D-1).
